column1: 1,Setup,"Use App Router project structure","Next.js App Router uses the app/ directory for file-based routing with React Server Components by default. Organize your project with a clear separation of app routes, components, and lib utilities.","Create an app/ directory with layout.tsx and page.tsx at the root
column2: place shared components in a components/ folder and utilities in lib/","Do not mix pages/ and app/ directories for the same routes
column3: do not put non-route files directly in app/ without a naming convention","// Recommended project structure

column1: //   utils.ts","// BAD: Mixing pages/ and app/ for same routes

2,Routing,"Use file-based routing with app directory","Next.js App Router maps the file system to URL paths. Each folder represents a route segment and page.tsx inside it makes the route publicly accessible. Use layout.tsx for shared UI across child routes.","Create a page.tsx file inside each route folder to define the route
column2: use layout.tsx for shared wrappers","Do not create routes without page.tsx
column3: do not use index.tsx naming (that is the Pages Router convention)","// app/about/page.tsx

3,Routing,"Use dynamic routes with [slug] and catch-all [...slug]","Dynamic segments are created by wrapping a folder name in square brackets [slug]. Catch-all segments use [...slug] and optional catch-all uses [[...slug]]. Access params via the params prop in server components.","Use [param] folders for single dynamic segments and [...param] for catch-all routes
column2: always validate params","Do not use query strings for path-based navigation
column3: do not forget to handle missing or invalid params","// app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx

column1: 4,Routing,"Use route groups (folder) for layout organization","Route groups created with parentheses in folder names like (marketing) let you organize routes without affecting the URL structure. They are useful for applying different layouts to different sections.","Use (groupName) folders to group related routes that share a layout
column2: use multiple root layouts via route groups","Do not create route groups that result in conflicting page.tsx files resolving to the same URL path","// app/(marketing)/layout.tsx

5,Server Components,"Use Server Components by default","In the App Router all components are React Server Components (RSC) by default. They run only on the server, can directly access databases and file systems, and send zero JavaScript to the client.","Keep components as server components unless they need browser APIs or interactivity
column2: fetch data directly in the component","Do not add ""use client"" to components that do not need interactivity
column3: do not import client-only libraries in server components without ""use client""","// app/users/page.tsx (Server Component by default)

column1: 6,Client Components,"Use ""use client"" directive for client-side interactivity","Add the ""use client"" directive at the top of files that need browser APIs, event handlers, useState, useEffect, or other React hooks. Keep client components as leaf nodes in the component tree for optimal performance.","Add ""use client"" only to components that require interactivity
column2: keep client components small and focused at the leaves of the tree","Do not mark entire pages as client components
column3: do not import server-only code in client components","// components/counter.tsx

column1: 7,Server Actions,"Use server actions for form handling","Server Actions are async functions that run on the server, defined with ""use server"". They can be used in form action attributes for progressive enhancement and work without JavaScript enabled on the client.","Define server actions with ""use server"" in a separate file or inline
column2: use them in form action props

8,Server Actions,"Use revalidatePath and revalidateTag after mutations","After performing a mutation with a server action call revalidatePath or revalidateTag to purge the cached data so the UI reflects the latest state. This integrates with the Next.js incremental cache.","Call revalidatePath for page-level cache clearing or revalidateTag for granular tag-based invalidation after mutations","Do not forget to revalidate after mutations
column2: do not revalidate overly broad paths that cause unnecessary cache purges","// app/posts/actions.ts

9,Data Fetching,"Use fetch() with caching in server components","In server components you can use the native fetch API with Next.js caching extensions. By default fetch results are cached. Use next.revalidate or cache: ""no-store"" to control caching behavior.","Use fetch() directly in server components with appropriate cache and revalidate options
column2: colocate data fetching with the component that uses it","Do not fetch data in client components when server components can do it
column3: do not forget to set revalidation for dynamic data","// app/products/page.tsx

column1: }","// BAD: Using client-side fetching for static data

10,Data Fetching,"Use parallel data fetching for performance","When a component needs multiple independent data sources fetch them in parallel using Promise.all instead of sequentially. This reduces total loading time by running requests concurrently.","Use Promise.all to fetch independent data in parallel
column2: start all promises before awaiting any of them","Do not await each fetch sequentially when the requests are independent
column3: do not create waterfall fetching patterns","// app/dashboard/page.tsx

column1: // Waits for user before starting analytics fetch

// Instead of max(user fetch, analytics fetch)

11,Caching,"Use request deduplication and cache strategies","Next.js automatically deduplicates fetch requests with the same URL and options during a single render pass. Understand the four caching layers: request memoization, data cache, full route cache, and router cache.","Rely on automatic request deduplication for the same fetch across components
column2: use cache: ""no-store"" for always-fresh data and next.revalidate for time-based revalidation","Do not manually implement request deduplication
column3: do not set cache: ""no-store"" on every fetch as it disables all caching benefits","// Automatic deduplication: both components share one request

column1: export default async function Layout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
// Same fetch in page is deduplicated automatically","// BAD: Disabling cache everywhere
cache: 'no-store', // Static data should be cached!

12,Caching,"Use unstable_cache and next.revalidate for granular caching","For non-fetch data sources like database queries use the unstable_cache wrapper (or the React cache function) to enable caching with tags and revalidation. Set next.revalidate on fetch for time-based invalidation.","Wrap database calls with unstable_cache and provide meaningful tags
column2: use next.revalidate with fetch for ISR-like behavior","Do not call the database without caching in production for read-heavy routes
column3: do not forget to add tags so you can revalidate granularly","// lib/data.ts

13,Middleware,"Use middleware for auth redirects and i18n","Next.js middleware runs before every matched request at the edge. Use it for authentication checks, redirects, internationalization, and request header manipulation. Define it in middleware.ts at the project root.","Create a middleware.ts at the project root

14,Layouts,"Use root layout and nested layouts","The root layout in app/layout.tsx is required and must include html and body tags. 15,Loading/Error,"Use loading.tsx and error.tsx conventions","Next.js uses special file conventions: loading.tsx creates an instant loading UI with React Suspense, error.tsx creates an error boundary that catches errors in the route segment. Both receive automatic wrapping.","Create loading.tsx for skeleton UIs during data fetching

16,API Routes,"Use Route Handlers for API endpoints","Route Handlers are defined in route.ts files inside the app directory. They support standard HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH) as named exports. They run on the server and can access all Node.js APIs.","Export named functions matching HTTP methods from route.ts files

20,Environment Variables,"Use NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix for client exposure","Next.js only exposes environment variables prefixed with NEXT_PUBLIC_ to the browser bundle. Server-only secrets must never have this prefix. Access server variables directly via process.env in server components.","Prefix client-needed variables with NEXT_PUBLIC_
